metadata.context: Official Sentry SDK for Python applications.

top_gaps.title: API Versioning Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No formal versioning strategy for the SDK's public API beyond semver on the package
top_gaps.impact: Breaking changes risk for downstream consumers without explicit API contract versioning

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity Integration
top_gaps.gap: No centralized IdP integration for CI/CD and release workflows — relies on GitHub-native auth
top_gaps.impact: Limited auditability and cross-system identity governance

top_gaps.title: Compute Hardening and Patching
top_gaps.gap: CI runners use default GitHub-hosted images with no custom hardening; no vulnerability scanning of CI environment
top_gaps.impact: Potential supply chain risk from unvetted CI environment

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: Secrets managed via GitHub Actions secrets but no rotation policy documented
top_gaps.impact: No automated rotation or centralized secrets lifecycle management

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: Dependency scanning via Dependabot present but no SAST tool integrated
top_gaps.impact: Code-level vulnerabilities may reach release without static analysis detection

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists — README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG.md, MIGRATION_GUIDE.md, and Sphinx docs/ directory
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A RAG-based knowledge agent that indexes the SDK documentation, migration guide, and changelog to answer developer questions about SDK usage, migration paths, and integration configuration.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate structured embeddings from the documentation corpus; consider converting Sphinx docs to a vector-indexed format.

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: OPS-Q1 >= 2 — The SDK itself implements distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry integration, sentry-trace propagation, span processing)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An observability agent that helps SDK users diagnose tracing issues, identify propagation gaps, and understand span hierarchies in their instrumented applications.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index the integration test outputs and tracing documentation to build context for the agent.

findings.title: Python SDK uses EOL minimum version (3.6) and legacy build system
findings.description: The SDK is written in Python supporting 3.6-3.14t. Modern tooling (ruff, mypy strict) is in use but the minimum supported version is Python 3.6 (EOL December 2021) and the build system uses legacy setup.py.
findings.gap: Minimum Python 3.6 is past EOL. Build system uses legacy setup.py rather than pyproject.toml build backend.
findings.recommendation: Drop Python 3.6-3.7 support. Migrate build system from setup.py to pyproject.toml with a modern build backend.

findings.title: Some integration paths use synchronous blocking patterns
findings.description: The SDK supports both sync and async patterns with background worker for event submission. Some integrations rely on synchronous blocking for event capture, limited by Python 3.6 minimum support.
findings.gap: Some integration paths perform synchronous network I/O. Python 3.6 minimum limits modern async adoption.
findings.recommendation: As Python 3.6-3.7 support is dropped, migrate remaining synchronous transport paths to native async where applicable.

findings.title: No formal API versioning strategy beyond package semver
findings.description: The SDK uses semantic versioning for package releases and has a MIGRATION_GUIDE.md, but lacks explicit public API surface declarations, stability tiers, or deprecation lifecycle policy.
findings.gap: No formal API stability tiers (stable vs experimental vs internal). No deprecation lifecycle policy documented in code.
findings.recommendation: Define explicit API stability tiers. Add a formal deprecation policy. Use __all__ consistently or a @public decorator to formalize the API surface.

findings.title: Integration auto-discovery lacks machine-readable catalog
findings.description: The SDK uses a plugin-based auto-discovery mechanism via import-time detection and entry_points. No machine-readable catalog of integrations with version compatibility exists.
findings.gap: No catalog or manifest listing all available integrations with their version compatibility matrix in a machine-readable format.
findings.recommendation: Publish a machine-readable integration catalog (JSON/YAML) documenting each integration's name, supported library versions, and feature matrix.

findings.title: Integration data capture patterns are inconsistent across modules
findings.description: The SDK has a centralized data pipeline (capture → scope → serialize → transport) but 65+ integration modules use varying patterns for data extraction (monkey-patching, hooks, decorators).
findings.gap: No unified Integration Data Protocol enforces consistency across integration modules.
findings.recommendation: Define a formal Integration Data Protocol standardizing how integrations capture spans, breadcrumbs, and context.

findings.title: Some database integration minimums include EOL library versions
findings.description: The SDK supports database drivers with minimum versions that include EOL releases: SQLAlchemy 1.2 (past EOL), pymongo 3.1 (legacy).
findings.gap: Minimum supported versions for some database integrations include EOL versions of underlying libraries.
findings.recommendation: Align minimum supported database driver versions with actively maintained versions. Bump minimums to sqlalchemy>=1.4, pymongo>=4.0.

findings.title: SQL query capture does not classify portable vs proprietary SQL
findings.description: The SDK captures raw SQL through database integrations but does not distinguish between portable SQL and proprietary/stored-procedure SQL in span descriptions.
findings.gap: No query classification metadata to distinguish portable SQL from proprietary extensions.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding query classification metadata to database spans indicating portable vs proprietary SQL patterns.

findings.title: DSN authentication uses static keys without rotation support
findings.description: The SDK authenticates to Sentry backend using DSN-based static public keys. No support for scoped or short-lived tokens.
findings.gap: DSN uses a static key rather than rotating tokens. No granular token-based auth support.
findings.recommendation: Consider supporting scoped, short-lived authentication tokens as an alternative to static DSNs for stricter environments.

findings.title: No centralized IdP integration for CI/CD workflows
findings.description: CI/CD and release pipelines use GitHub-native auth mechanisms (GitHub App tokens, repository secrets). No centralized enterprise IdP federation.
findings.gap: CI/CD identity is GitHub-native with no centralized enterprise IdP federation.
findings.recommendation: For an open-source library, GitHub-native auth is standard. Document how DSN authentication maps to enterprise IAM patterns for enterprise consumers.

findings.title: Secrets managed via GitHub Actions but no rotation policy
findings.description: No plaintext credentials in the repository. All secrets managed through GitHub Actions encrypted secrets. No documented rotation policy or automated rotation.
findings.gap: No documented rotation policy or automated rotation mechanism for CI secrets.
findings.recommendation: Document a secrets rotation schedule. Use OIDC-based trusted publishers for all external service authentication where supported.

findings.title: CI runners use default images with no hardening or vulnerability scanning
findings.description: CI workflows run on GitHub-hosted runners with default images. SHA-pinned actions provide supply-chain security, but no custom hardening or vulnerability scanning of CI environment.
findings.gap: No vulnerability scanning of CI runner environment. No hardened base images.
findings.recommendation: Add container scanning for the Python 3.6 Docker container used in CI. Consider verifying runner image provenance.

findings.title: No SAST tool in CI pipeline
findings.description: Dependabot and FOSSA provide dependency scanning and license compliance. No dedicated SAST tool for code-level security vulnerability detection.
findings.gap: No SAST tool configured as a pipeline gate for code-level security vulnerabilities.
findings.recommendation: Add Bandit or Semgrep with Python security rules to the CI pipeline as a required check.

findings.title: SDK lacks internal telemetry for its own operations
findings.description: The SDK implements comprehensive distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry propagator, sentry-trace headers, span processing) for consuming applications but does not instrument its own internal operations.
findings.gap: No internal telemetry for SDK performance (serialization time, transport latency, queue depth) exposed to host application.
findings.recommendation: Add optional internal SDK telemetry that consumers can enable to monitor SDK health.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and the pathway requires a deployable workload.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and the pathway requires a deployable workload.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 < 3 AND commercial DB engines detected. DATA-Q4=3, no commercial engines.

triggering_questions.note: DATA-Q4=3 meets threshold; no commercial DB engines detected

pathways.not_triggered_reason: DATA-Q4 scores 3 (meets threshold) and no commercial database engines are used by this library.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and the pathway requires database infrastructure.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and the pathway requires data processing workloads.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library'. Libraries use build/publish pipelines, not deployment pipelines.

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Requires AI/agent/LLM intent in context. Context describes 'Official Sentry SDK for Python applications' — observability SDK, not AI application.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The SDK provides AI integrations (OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain) but serves as an observability tool, not an AI application.
